results.rendered_html:
ДҒrya 1гҖү Z. 12. fg. streiche die Stelle aus  AIT. BR.  und vgl. yuvamДҒrin.
вҖ” aгҖү entspricht hГӨufig unserm Herr, so z. B.  VARДҖH. Bб№ҡH. S. 51,23 .
вҖ” cгҖү  HALДҖY. 2,415 .
вҖ” fгҖү N. pr. eines Mannes  Verz. d. Oxf. H. 371,b, No. 248 .
вҖ” gгҖү ein WГӨchter im Gynaeceum  HALДҖY. 5,28 .
вҖ” 3гҖүcгҖү eine ДҖryДҒ-Strophe: ДҒryДҒmimДҒб№ғ jagau  KATHДҖS. 52,158 . 53,31 . 56,358 .
вҖ” dгҖү Titel zweier im ДҖryДҒ-Metrum verfasster Schriftchen  HALL 146 . 151 .

results.rendered_html:
ДҒrya (von 1. arya), nach  P. 6,2,58 , Sch. ДҒrya,
1гҖү m. f. ДҒryДҒ der zu den Treuen, Ergebenen GehГ¶rige, d. h. der Mann des eigenen Stammes; zuweilen auch wohl gedacht als der den VolksgГ¶ttern des Stammes Treue; N., mit welchem die indischen wie die iranischen StГӨmme sich benannten: Arier (Gegens. dasyu und dДҒsa). SpГӨter (mit dem Gegens. ЕӣЕ«dra) ist Arier вҖ” der AngehГ¶rige des in Indien herrschenden Stammes, der Mann der drei oberen Kasten. vi jДҒnД«hyДҒryДҒnye ca dasyavaбёҘ  б№ҡV. 1,51,8 . yajamДҒnamДҒryam 130,8 . 156,5 . ahaб№ғ bhЕ«mimadadДҒmДҒryДҒyДҒhaб№ғ vб№ӣб№Јб№ӯiб№ғ dДҒЕӣuб№Јe martyДҒya 4,26,2 . yo vДҒryДҒtsapta sindhuб№Јu . vardhardДҒsasya tuvinб№ӣmб№Үa nД«namaбёҘ der Arier im Lande der sieben StrГ¶me 8,24,27 . sДҒhyДҒma dДҒsamДҒryam 10,83,1 . 1,59,2 . 117,21 . 2,11,18 . 19 . 6,18,3 . 7,5,6 . 8,92,1 . 10,138,3 .  VДҖLAKH. 3,9 . yaЕӣca ЕӣЕ«dra utДҒryaбёҘ  AV. 4,20,4 . 8 . 19,32,8 . 62,1 .  VS. 14,30 .  NIR. 2,2 . ayuvamДҒryasya rДҒб№Јб№ӯraб№ғ bhavati  AIT. BR. 8,25 .  KДҖTY. ЕҡR. 13,3,7 . 8 . 4,14,1 . mukhabДҒhЕ«rupajjДҒnДҒб№ғ yДҒ loke jДҒtayo bahiбёҘ . mlecchavДҒcДҒЕӣcДҒryavДҒcaбёҘ sarve te dasyavaбёҘ smб№ӣtДҒбёҘ ..  M. 10,45 . varб№ҮДҒpetamavijГұДҒtaб№ғ naraб№ғ kaluб№Јayonijam . ДҒryarЕ«pamivДҒnДҒryaб№ғ karmabhiбёҘ svairvibhДҒvayet .. 57 . jДҒto nДҒryДҒmanДҒryДҒyДҒmДҒryДҒdДҒryo bhavedguб№ҮaiбёҘ . jДҒto 'pyanДҒryДҒdДҒryДҒyДҒmanДҒrya iti niЕӣcayaбёҘ .. 67 . anДҒryamДҒryakarmДҒб№ҮamДҒryaб№ғ cДҒnДҒryakarmiб№Үam . saб№ғpradhДҒryДҒbravД«ddhДҒtДҒ na samau nДҒsamДҒviti .. 73 . 7,69 . antyasyДҒryДҒgame vadhaбёҘ  YДҖJГ‘. 2,294 . mlecchДҒЕӣcДҒnye bahuvidhДҒбёҘ pЕ«rvaб№ғ ye nikб№ӣtДҒ raб№Үe . ДҒryДҒЕӣca pб№ӣthivД«pДҒlДҒбёҘ  MBH. 14,2137 . Гјberh. ein Mann, der besonderer Achtung werth ist,  AK. 1,1,7,14 .  H. 333 . 379 . an. 2,345 . kathaб№ғ dДҒsasya dДҒsД« tvaб№ғ bhДҒryДҒ bhavitumarhasi . yo 'hamДҒryeб№Үa paravДҒnbhrДҒtrДҒ jyeб№Јб№ӯhena bhДҒvini ..  R. 3,24,9 . In den folgg. Еҡloka wird der ГӨltere Bruder schlechtweg ДҒrya genannt. ДҒryasaб№ғsadi  M. 8,75 . 179 . 395 .  PAГ‘CAT. I, 86 . HГӨufig in der Anrede (ДҒrya, ДҒrye)  N. 12,16 .  HIбёҢ. 4,30 .  BRДҖHMAб№Ҷ. 2,28 .  ЕҡДҖK. 3,6 . 11 . 15 . 4,11 . 16  (vgl.  BHARATA _zu_ ЕҡДҖK. 1 .: vДҒcyau naб№ӯД«sЕ«tradharДҒvДҒryanДҒmnДҒ parasparam).  HIT. 8,19 . 45,1 . unter Thieren in der Fabel 18,16 .  PAГ‘CAT. 32,8 . Kann in vielen von diesen Beispielen auch als adj. gefasst werden. Insbes. heisst noch ДҒrya
aгҖү der Gebieter  H. 359  (nach  ЕҡKDR. ; vgl. 1. arya 2);
bгҖү der Freund  AJAYAP. _im_ ЕҡKDR. ;
cгҖү der VaiЕӣya  H. 864  (vgl. 2. arya);
dгҖү Buddha  H. 232 ;
eгҖү buddh. ein Mann, der Гјber die vier Grundwahrheiten nachgedacht hat und sein Betragen darnach richtet (Gegens. pб№ӣthagjana),  BURN. Intr. 290. fg. ;
fгҖү ein Sohn des Manu SДҒvarб№Үa  HARIV. 465 .
вҖ” 2гҖү adj. f. ДҒryДҒ und ДҒrД« (ved.) arisch: devayantД«rviЕӣa upa bruvate dasmamДҒrД«бёҘ die arischen Gemeinen, StГӨmme  б№ҡV. 1,77,3 . 96,31 . sahaбёҘ 103,3 . hatvД« dasyЕ«nprДҒryaб№ғ varб№ҮamДҒvat 3,34,9 . na yo rara ДҒryaб№ғ nДҒma dasyave 10,49,3 . viЕӣvamДҒryam 9,63,5 . dhДҒmДҒnyДҒryДҒ 14 . ДҒryДҒ vratДҒ visб№ӣjanto adhi kб№Јami arische Herrschaft auf Erden verbreitend 10,65,11 . yadД« viЕӣo vб№ӣб№Үate dasmamДҒryДҒ agniб№ғ hotДҒram 11,4 . 43,4 . [Page1-0697] Indra und Agni heissen arisch, d. h. den Ariern gewogen  б№ҡV. 6,60,6 : hato vб№ӣtrДҒб№ҮyДҒryДҒ hato dasДҒni satpatД«, wofern nicht hier ДҒryДҒ als ursprГјngliche Betonung angenommen werden muss: ihr schlaget die arischen und die fremden Feinde; vgl. dДҒsДҒ vб№ӣtrДҒб№ҮyДҒryДҒ ca 6,33,3 . dДҒsДҒ ca vб№ӣtrДҒ hatamДҒryДҒб№Үi ca 7,83,1 . 10,69,6 . SpГӨter: sich wie ein Arier betragend, eines Ariers wГјrdig, ehrenhaft, ehrenwerth, edel: guruputreб№Јu cДҒryeб№Јu  M. 2,207 .  R. 3,2,13 . navaб№ғ vб№ӣttamДҒryamanusmaran 5,36,66 . mДҒrgamДҒryaб№ғ prapannasya 2,115,6 . ДҒryДҒб№ғ matiб№ғ kб№ӣtvДҒ 6,16,72 . ДҒryamati  SДҖб№ӮKHYAK. 71 . strД«б№ҮДҒmДҒryasvabhДҒvДҒnДҒm  R. 3,2,23 . manas  ЕҡДҖK. 21 . vor einem nom. pr.  ЕҡДҖK. 80,23 . Bei den Buddhisten erhГӨlt fast jedes Werk das Beiwort ДҒrya. Nach den Lexicographen bedeutet ДҒrya adj.: aus guter Familie stammend  AK. 2,7,2 . ehrwГјrdig (pЕ«jya), vorzГјglich (Еӣreб№Јб№ӯha), verstГӨndig (buddha)  ЕҡABDAR. _im_ ЕҡKDR.  entsprechend (saб№ғgata)  AJAYA _im_ ЕҡKDR. 
вҖ” 3гҖү f.
aгҖү f. zu arya 1; s. daselbst.
вҖ” bгҖү ein Bein. der PДҒrvatД«  TRIK. 1,1,51 .  H. 203 . an. 2,346 .  HARIV. 3270 .
вҖ” cгҖү ein bes. Metrum (aus 12 + 18 und 12 + 17 Moren bestehend)  H. an. 2,346 .  ЕҡRUT. 4 .  COLEBR. Misc. Ess. II, 72 . 153 . Д«Еӣvarakб№ӣб№Јб№Үena caitadДҒryДҒbhiбёҘ . saб№ғkб№Јiptam  SДҖб№ӮKHYAK. 71 .  HORДҖЕҡ. _in_ Ind. St. 2,277 .
вҖ” Vgl. anДҒrya.

results.rendered_html:
ДҒrya a. [б№ӣ-б№Үyat]
1 ДҖryan.
2 Worthy of an ДҖrya.
3 Worthy, venerable, respectable, honourable, noble, high; yadДҒryamasyДҒmabhilДҒб№Јi me manaбёҘ  Еҡ. 1. 22 ; so ДҒryaveб№ЈaбёҘ respectable dress; oft. used in theatrical language as an honorific adjective and a respectful mode of address; ДҒryacДҒб№ҮakyaбёҘ, ДҒryДҒ aruб№ғdhatД« &c.; ДҒrya revered or honoured Sir; ДҒrye revered or honoured lady. The following rules are laid down for the use of ДҒrya in addressing persons:- (1) vДҒcyau naб№ӯД«sЕ«tradhДҒrДҒvДҒryanДҒmnДҒ parasparaб№ғ . (2) vayasyetyuttamairvДҒcyo mavyairДҒryeti cДҒgrajaбёҘ . (3) ( vaktavyo) amДҒtya ДҒryeti ghetaraiбёҘ . (4) svecchayДҒ nДҒmamiviб№ғpraiviб№ғpra ДҒryeti cetaraiбёҘ .  S. D. 431. 
4 Noble, fine, excellent.
вҖ” ryaбёҘ
1 N. of the Hindu and Iranian people, as distinguished from anДҒrya, dasyu and dДҒsaбёҘ vijДҒnД«hyДҒryДҒvye ca darayavaбёҘ  Rv. 1. 51. 8. 
2 A man who is faithful to the religion and laws of his country; kartavyamДҒcaran kДҒryamakartavyamanДҒcaran . tiб№Јб№ӯhati prakб№ӣtДҒcДҒre sa vДҒ ДҒrya iti smб№ӣtaбёҘ ...
3 N. of the first three castes (as opp. to ЕӣЕ«dra).
4 A respectable or honourable man, esteemed person; vб№ӣttena hi bhavatyДҒryo na dhanena na vidyayДҒ  Mb. 
5 A man of noble birth.
6 A man of noble character.
7 A master, owner.
8 A preceptor.
9 A friend.
10
(pb) A VaiЕӣya.
11 A father-in-law (as in ДҒryapuб№ғtra).
12 A Buddha.
13 (With the Buddhists) A man who has thought on the four chief principles of Buddhism and lives according to them.
14 A son of Manu SДҒvarб№Үa.
вҖ” ryДҒ
1 N. of PДҒrvatД«.
2 A mother-in-law.
3 A respectable woman.
4 N. of a metre, see Appendix.
Comp.
вҖ” aб№Јб№ӯaЕӣataб№ғ title of a work of ДҖrya Bhaб№ӯб№ӯa consisting of eight hundred verses.
вҖ” ДҒvartaбёҘ [ДҒryДҒ ДҒvartaб№ғte atra] вҖҳabode of the noble or excellent (ДҖryas);вҖҷ particularly N. of the tract extending from the eastern to the western ocean, and bounded on the north and south by the HimДҒlaya and Vindhya respectively; cf.  Ms. 2. 22 ; ДҒsamudrДҒttu vai pЕ«rvДҒdДҒsamudrДҒcca paЕӣcimДҒt . tayorevДҒб№ғtaraб№ғ giryoбёҘ (himavadviб№ғdhyayoбёҘ) ДҒryДҒvartaб№ғ vidurbudhДҒбёҘ; also 10. 34. 
вҖ” gб№ӣhya a. [ДҒryasya gб№ӣhyaбёҘ pakб№ЈaбёҘ]
1 to be respected by the noble
2 a friend of the noble, readily accessible to honourable men; tamДҒryagб№ӣhyaб№ғ nigб№ӣhД«tadhenuбёҘ  R. 2. 33. 
3 respectable, right, decorous.
вҖ” deЕӣaбёҘ a country inhabited by the ДҖryas.
вҖ” putraбёҘ
1 son of an honourable man.
2 the son of a spiritual preceptor.
3 honorific designation of the son of the elder brother; of a husband by his wife; or of a prince by his general &c.
4 the son of the father-in-law, i. e. a husband (occurring in every drama; mostly in the vocative case in the last two senses).
вҖ” prДҒya a.
1 inhabited by the ДҖryas.
2 abounding with respectable people.
вҖ” bhaб№ӯб№ӯaбёҘ N. of a renowned astronomer, the inventor of Algebra among the Hindus; he flourished before the 5th century of the Christian era.
вҖ” bhДҒvaбёҘ honourable character or behaviour.
вҖ” mДҒrgaбёҘ the path or course of the respectable, a respectable way.
вҖ” miЕӣra a. respectable, worthy, distinguished.
вҖ” (ЕӣraбёҘ) a gentleman, a man of consequence; (pl.)
1 worthy or respectable men, an assembly of honourable men; ДҒryamiЕӣrДҒn vijГұДҒpayДҒmi  V. 1. 
2 your reverence or honour (a respectful address); nanvДҒryamiЕӣraiбёҘ prathamameva ДҒjГұaptaб№ғ  Еҡ. 1 ; ДҒryamiЕӣrДҒбёҘ pramДҒб№Үaб№ғ  M. 1. 
вҖ” yuvan m. an ДҖryan youth.
вҖ” rЕ«pa a. having only the form of an ДҖryan, a hypocrite,
(pb) impostor.
вҖ” liб№ғgin m. an impostor;  Ms. 9. 260. 
вҖ” vб№ӣtta a. virtuous, good, pious;  Ms. 9. 253 ;  R. 14. 55. 
вҖ” (ttaб№ғ) the conduct of an ДҖryan or nobleman;  Ms. 4. 175. 
вҖ” veЕӣa a. well-clothed, having a respectable dress, fine.
вҖ” vrata a. observing the laws and ordinances of the ДҖryans or noblemen.
вҖ” (taб№ғ) the duty of an ДҖryan.
вҖ” ЕӣvetaбёҘ a noble or honourable man.
вҖ” saб№ғghaбёҘ the whole body of the ДҖryans.
вҖ” satyaб№ғ a noble or sublime truth; (there are four such truths forming the chief principles of Buddhism).
вҖ” halaб№ғ ind. [cf.  P. I. 1. 47 ] forcibly.
вҖ” hб№ӣdya a. liked by the noble.
